questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'echo' command in Unix?
options: B) To list files and directories
A) To display a message on the screen
C) To change the current directory
D) To delete files and directories
questions.explanation: The 'echo' command is used to display a message on the screen.

questions.question: Which command is used to display the current date and time?
options: B) Date
A) Time
C) Cal
D) Who
questions.explanation: The 'date' command is used to display the current date and time.

questions.question: What is the output of the command 'ls -l'?
options: B) A list of files and directories in a plain text format
A) A list of files and directories in a detailed format
C) A list of files and directories in a graphical format
D) A list of files and directories in a hidden format
questions.explanation: The command 'ls -l' displays a list of files and directories in a detailed format.

questions.question: Which command is used to display the username of the current user?
options: B) Who
A) Passwd
C) Cal
D) Date
questions.explanation: The 'who' command is used to display the username of the current user.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'cal' command in Unix?
options: B) To display the current date and time
A) To display a calendar for a specific month
C) To change the current directory
D) To delete files and directories
questions.explanation: The 'cal' command is used to display a calendar for a specific month.

questions.question: Which command is used to combine the output of multiple commands?
options: B) | (pipe)
A) & (ampersand)
C) > (greater than)
D) < (less than)
questions.explanation: The '|' character is used to pipe the output of one command as the input to another command.
